key_insight: Spray method matters as much as composition: HVOF is better for carbide coatings, while detonation spraying performs better for Al2O3-NiCr.
engineering_relevance: The study helps engineers select the coating process that best balances density, hardness, and friction performance for wear-resistant surfaces.
method: Compared WC-NiCr, Cr3C2-NiCr, and Al2O3-NiCr coatings
Applied coatings using HVOF and detonation spraying
Evaluated morphology, microstructure, microhardness, and tribology
evidence_signal: SEM/EDS, FISCHERSCOPE microhardness, and ball-on-disc tribology tests (ASTM G133-95 and ASTM G99) showed clear process-dependent differences in coating structure and performance.
watch_item: Results are process- and composition-specific, so transferability to other powders, substrates, or spray parameters may be limited without re-optimization.
paper_title: <b>Comparative analysis of WC-NiCr, AL₂O₃-NiCr, and CR₃C₂-NiCr metal-ceramic coatings applied by HVOF and detonation spraying methods</b>
